text:
$AEHR  Aehr Test Systems

TLDR 5 reasons why AEHR will outperform the market
-Its tools stress semiconductors exposing failures before they become far more costly downstream.
-Customers are increasingly interested in components that will not fail
-One quarter of bookings exceeded the entire previous year’s revenue.
-AEHR orders should lead the optical revenue curve by several quarters.
-A bet in AEHR is a bet that optical test demand follows an S-curve over the next 12 months.

Napkin math:

• FY2026 revenue: $50M
• 2028 revenue ceiling: $471M (9.4x)
• Market cap: $2.3B at $72 close, ~$2.9B at ~$92 AH
• 2028 target: ~$5.7B / $177 per share
• Upside: 2.4x at close, ~1.9x AH

1/ Aehr builds wafer-level and package-level burn-in systems.
Its tools stress semiconductors before they enter expensive optical modules, AI accelerators and power systems, exposing latent failures before they become far more costly downstream.

2/ AI infrastructure is approaching the limits of copper.
1.6T optics, CPO and increasingly optical scale-up networks require massive volumes of lasers, photonic chips and optical engines.

Those devices need production test and burn-in capacity before they can ship.

3/ Aehr’s FOX platform burns in entire wafers in parallel.

That allows customers to remove weak devices before packaging and creates recurring demand for proprietary WaferPak contactors as the installed base expands.

-=-=-=-=-=-
AEHR orders should lead the optical revenue curve by several quarters.
-=-=-=-=-=-

4/ The July print confirmed the inflection:

• Q4 revenue: $18.8M
• Record bookings: $60.7M
• Effective backlog: $100.6M
• FY2027 guidance: $130M–$150M
• Non-GAAP net margin guide: 18%–22%

One quarter of bookings exceeded the entire previous year’s revenue.

https://t.co/9hGWkjfYZW

5/ The optical evidence matters more than the headline guide.
• Lead silicon-photonics customer is ramping
• Second global networking customer forecasts more systems this year
• Lead AI customer is moving accelerator burn-in from system level to all wafer level
• Another top-tier AI processor supplier completed successful benchmarking and requested pilot validation

6/ My 2028 revenue ceiling:

• Silicon-photonics WLBI: ~$225M
• AI-processor WLBI and Sonoma: ~$196M
• SiC, GaN and other: ~$50M
• Total: ~$471M
The margin path rises from 18% in Q3 2026 toward 40% in 2028 as volume and proprietary consumables scale.

7/ The market historically treated Aehr as a volatile SiC equipment company.
The new order book is increasingly driven by AI processors and silicon photonics.
The remaining alpha comes from estimating how quickly $100.6M of backlog and the optical qualification pipeline convert into revenue.

8/ Supply-chain position:

InP lasers: $LITE $COHR $SIVE

SiPh PICs: $TSEM
→ Wafer-level burn-in: $AEHR
→ Optical engines/modules: $AAOI $COHR $LITE
→ AI switches and processors: $NVDA $AVGO $MRVL

Test capacity must be installed before this chain can reach volume.

9/ Risks:
• Customer concentration remains high
• My $170M FY2027 ceiling exceeds the $150M guide
• Order recognition can move between quarters
• Sonoma competes with Advantest and Teradyne
• Q4 still showed a GAAP operating loss
• At ~$92 AH, the stock has little tolerance for a slow conversion
Counter-evidence: two photonics customers, a second AI-processor evaluation and record backlog.

10/ Catalysts:

• Additional FOX-XP orders from either photonics customer
• Pilot-production order from the newly benchmarked AI supplier
• FY2027 revenue tracking above $150M
• Consumables returning above 30% of revenue
• Operating-margin progression through each quarter

At ~$92, this is no longer a cheap discovery trade.
It is a bet that optical test demand follows an S-curve over the next 12 months.

I think it does, expectation is about a 2x over the next 12 months (from 92 to about 200)

text:
$AEHR has been a top-five position for me since $10 because I saw it evolving from a struggling silicon carbide equipment supplier into a critical reliability platform for photonics, memory and power semiconductors.

That inflection is now happening with backlog nearly doubling since February and FY27 guidance implying 160%–200% growth across AI accelerators, silicon photonics and next-generation networking.

Shares are now up more than 30% after hours.

text:
$TSM volume เยอะมากกกกก ผลิตไม่ทันครับ ทำให้หลาย hyperscaler ต้องหา Plan B  NPO บนแพลตฟอร์มของ $TSEM

$TSEM มี moat SiPho platform ของตัวเองด้วย ไม่ใช่แค่ OEM อย่างเดียว และ capacity ล้นต้องการ fab เพิ่ม

ต้นน้ำ $IQE => $TSEM
“ IQE ’s InP epiwafers will be used in several of Tower’s advanced silicon photonics platforms for next-generation optical technologies.”

- จาก press release ร่วมครัช

ใช้ผลิต 200G lane pluggable transceivers และอื่นๆ
ใช้ใน Optical Circuit Switches สำหรับ datacenter
TSEM ให้ IP แก่ IQE ด้วย license porous silicon patents

ซิลิคอนเก่งเรื่อง นำแสง (waveguide), ปรับแสง (modulator) และ ตรวจจับแสง (photodetector) มาก แต่ สร้างแสง (laser) ไม่ค่อยเก่ง เพราะโครงสร้างทางฟิสิกส์
InP เก่งเรื่องสร้างแสงมาก จึงต้องนำ InP มา heterogeneous integration กับ silicon platform ของ Tower เพื่อให้ได้ระบบที่สมบูรณ์แบบครับ

ส่วน $MRVL => $TSEM 
ไม่เกี่ยวกับข่าว plan B แต่ TSEM ผลิตของ MRVL 
volume เยอะมากๆๆและจะเยอะมหาศาลขึ้นอีก

text:
$AEHR The AI Thesis Keeps Playing Out Annnd Now SiC Is Reviving Too!!

AEHR guided FY2027 revenue to $ 130-150M. That’s 160-200% YoY growth, backed by a record ~$100.6 M effective backlog, not optimism, but visibility. 

They also expect non-GAAP net income at 18-22% of revenue.

Q4 already showed the swing: $18.8M revenue (+33% YoY), turned profitable ($1.4M GAAP / $3.6M non-GAAP), record $60.7M in bookings, cash jumped to $116.5 M.

The AI engine is confirming and expanding:
• Lead production customer ramping wafer-level burn-in for AI accelerators (training + inference) a structural content increase per chip.

• New top-tier AI supplier benchmark win now moving to pilot on a second device.

• Record follow-on Sonoma orders from lead hyperscaler for next-gen higher-power packages.

• Silicon photonics emerging as a third leg (new global networking customer + systems for optical I/O & data-center interconnects).

• Memory roadmap targeting NAND and potential high-bandwidth memory burn-in.

At the same time, the SiC business the market wrote off is coming back:

• >$8M new SiC wafer-level burn-in orders in the past month, including a direct order from one of the world’s largest automakers + first SiC customer in Taiwan.

This is no longer AI replacing SiC. It’s AI compounding while SiC recovers as a second engine underneath. Diversified across AI accelerators, photonics, memory, data-center power, with the EV-driven SiC business finally turning up.

Strong setup for a small-cap semi name with this level of visibility. The real risk is concentration in the lead AI customer. 

Key questions on the call: how much of the backlog sits with that one customer, is the benchmark win a new logo, and how the backlog splits across AI / SiC / photonics.

text:
$AEHR The Guidance: Management officially projected FY2027 revenue to be between $130 million and $150 million.
 Why it matters: Wall Street analysts were only modeling ~$82 million. AEHR's actual forecast represents a staggering 160% to 200% year-over-year growth trajectory.
 Profitability Margin: They also guided that non-GAAP net income will landing at 18% to 22% of total revenue.
What This Tells Us
The massive record bookings ($60.7 million) and total effective backlog ($100.6 million) prove that their pivot into custom ASIC package-level burn-in for hyperscale AI customers and silicon photonics isn't just a pilot project—it's scaling into high-volume manufacturing exceptionally fast. The supply chain bottleneck for AI infrastructure testing is officially paying off for them.

text:
$AEHR is up 30% after hours. 

It's certainly been a bumpy ride for photonics, but I think this ER is extremely bullish for the sector.  Congrats to the 3000+ investors who own this stock with me on @joinautopilot via my Photonics Is Next portfolio.

When @KawzInvests and I published our photonics testing deep dive, $AEHR had just printed a $10.3M revenue quarter and the market called it a miss. We said to watch the bookings, not the revenue.

Q4 settled it:

- Revenue of $18.8M, up 33% YoY
- Record bookings of $60.7M in a single quarter
- Effective backlog doubled in 90 days
- Back to GAAP profitability

Then the bomb. FY27 revenue guidance of $130M to $150M. That's 160% to 200% growth against $50M this year.

The drivers are exactly what we wrote about:

- Their lead AI customer is shifting all burn-in from system level to wafer level for its accelerators

- A benchmark with a major supplier of AI accelerators and CPUs exceeded expectations. They're now moving toward pilot production and handed $AEHR a second device to evaluate

- The lead silicon photonics customer is ramping with follow-on orders, and their newest customer, a global networking leader, is forecasting additional systems this year.

Remember the structural setup. Every silicon photonics wafer needs burn-in before packaging. $AEHR is still the only company doing this at production scale.

Everyone staring at 60x trailing sales missed that the guide compresses it to ~21x forward.

Super excited for photonics earnings season!

text:
$AEHR Q4 Performance & FY27 Outlook

​Financials

• ​Q4 Revenue: $19M (In-line).
• ​Q4 EPS: $0.11 (Beat vs. $0.01 loss estimate).

• ​Backlog: Strong position at $100M with $61M in new bookings.

• ​FY27 Guidance: $140M revenue (+180% YoY), significantly exceeding the $85M consensus.

​Key Drivers

• ​Silicon Photonics: Expanded to two customers, including a global networking leader, as AI data centers adopt optical I/O.

• ​Silicon Carbide: Added $8M in recent WaferPak orders, headlined by a top-three global automaker.

• ​Hyperscale (Sonoma): Record follow-on orders with a shift toward higher-power density devices.

The DustPhotonics acquisition brings silicon photonics in-house, a massive edge as copper constraints intensify. $LITE - Laser & Switching Leader in EMLs and optical circuit switching. Backed by a $2B strategic investment from $NVDA with an optical switch backlog stretching into 2028. $COHR - InP Infrastructure Addressing the critical Indium Phosphide bottleneck. Holds a $2B $NVDA commitment and is scaling capacity aggressively for 1.6T+ networking. $CIEN - Transport Layer The backbone that ties everything together. Their solutions enable hyperscalers to move enormous AI traffic between data centers as clusters scale to gigawatt levels. $FN - Manufacturing Backbone The "picks-and-shovels" partner. As the reliable assembly giant for optical modules, they fuel the entire ramp where speed and scale determine the winners. $MRVL - DSP & Electro-Optics Strengthening the infrastructure with PAM DSPs and custom chips. The Celestial AI deal and NVLink exposure place them at the heart of next-gen cluster design. $AEHR - Testing & Reliability Providing essential wafer-level burn-in (FOX-XP). Recently secured a major leader in networking for 1.6T production ramp (critical for hyperscale reliability). The transition from copper is no longer optional 😬 Anyway these stocks are volatile, with risks around customer concentration, production ramps (InP/SiPho), and high valuations. DYOR. text:
$AEHR delivers: Record $60.7M Q4 bookings, $100M + effective backlog, and FY2027 outlook of $130 - 150M — 2.6–3.0x fiscal 2026 revenue. Strong execution in AI processors and silicon photonics positions the company at the intersection of wafer-level test innovation and hyperscale infrastructure demand.

A name included in the Photonics basket , +32% aftermarket. Structural tailwinds building. 
$AEHR

Aehr Test Systems ($AEHR) — Ρεκόρ παραγγελιών, ισχυρό backlog και αισιόδοξο FY27 outlook

📊 Αποτελέσματα

Έσοδα: $18,8 εκατ. (+33,3% σε ετήσια βάση)
GAAP EPS: $0,04, έναντι -$0,10 πέρυσι
Non-GAAP EPS: $0,11, έναντι -$0,01 πέρυσι
Ρεκόρ παραγγελιών (Bookings): $60,7 εκατ.
Effective Backlog: $100,6 εκατ.

🎯 Guidance (FY27)

FY27 Revenue: $130M–$150M
Expected Revenue Growth: +160% έως +200% σε ετήσια βάση
FY27 Non-GAAP Net Income: 18%–22% του revenue

📌 Βασικά σημεία

Η ζήτηση για AI processors επιταχύνεται τόσο στο wafer-level burn-in όσο και στο package-level burn-in.
Μεγάλος προμηθευτής AI processors ολοκλήρωσε benchmark testing και εξετάζει την έναρξη πιλοτικής παραγωγής.
Πελάτες στον χώρο του silicon photonics αυξάνουν την παραγωγική τους δυναμικότητα για εφαρμογές AI optical interconnects.
Η Aehr έλαβε περίπου $8 εκατ. νέες παραγγελίες για silicon carbide μέσα στον τελευταίο μήνα.
Τα διαθέσιμα μετρητά και τα restricted cash αυξήθηκαν στα $116,5 εκατ.

💬 Σχόλιο διοίκησης

«Με πολλούς πελάτες να εισέρχονται ή να επεκτείνουν την παραγωγή τους, με ρεκόρ backlog και με πρόσθετες ευκαιρίες τόσο στο wafer-level όσο και στο package-level burn-in, πιστεύουμε ότι η Aehr είναι τοποθετημένη για αρκετά χρόνια ισχυρής ανάπτυξης εσόδων.»

text:
$AEHR just guided FY2027 revenue to $130M - $150M, up 160% to 200% from the $50M it printed in FY2026. 

Q4 came in at $18.8M with $0.11 adjusted EPS against a consensus loss, record bookings of $60.7M and an effective backlog of $100.6M 

The driver is silicon photonics burn in finally hitting production scale. Aehr’s lead SiPho customer keeps placing follow on orders for fully automated wafer level burn in systems, and a second major networking customer is ramping capacity. 

Every 800G/1.6T transceiver and optical I/O chip going into AI data centers needs reliability screening, and Aehr owns that step of the process.

This is the test layer of the optical interconnect buildout monetizing. Transceiver volume growth flows straight through to burn in capacity demand, and with AI processors and power semis stacked on top, the backlog gives unusual visibility for a company this size.

Stock seems to like it too

Up 28% so far.

text:
2027 Early Career Ambassador applications are now open! 

Apply here: https://t.co/j1aCzEsOB1

#EarlyCareer #Photonics #Leadership https://t.co/YTK4C9lVi8

$AEHR delivered another powerful confirmation that the AI infrastructure boom continues to expand. This time, confirmation came from the production-test layer of the semiconductor supply chain.

Q4 revenue rose 34% to $18.8 million, adjusted EPS reached $0.11 versus a year-ago loss, gross margin expanded 1,000 basis points to 45%, and bookings surged more than 500% to a record $60.7 million. Effective backlog now stands near $101 million, supporting fiscal 2027 revenue guidance of $130-150 million, representing 160%-200% growth, and roughly 66% above consensus at the midpoint.

AI processors generated approximately 71% of annual revenue, silicon photonics contributed another 20%, and together they accounted for more than 80% of Q4 sales.

Aehr's lead AI processor customer doubled its installed systems and shifted all production screening to wafer-level burn-in. A hyperscaler placed a $41 million order supporting high-volume AI-chip production. A separate top-tier accelerator, CPU, and networking supplier completed a benchmark that exceeded expectations and now wants to move a current high-volume device into pilot production. Crucially, guidance largely excludes that new customer, HBM, and high-bandwidth flash, leaving meaningful upside.

That's a "good-as-it-gets" quarter. Stacked up against what $ASML $TSM and Samsung have said in the last two weeks, we're getting confirmation of fundamental strength across the whole AI supply stack. ASML confirms equipment orders and fab expansion, TSMC confirms record advanced-chip production, Samsung confirms booming memory demand, and Aehr confirms those chips and optical interconnects are entering volume manufacturing.

That full-chain acceleration is difficult to reconcile with "peak spending" fears. The remaining question is still hyperscaler spending durability. That's why AI stocks still aren't catching a bid. If $META $AMZN $GOOGL $MSFT etc. reaffirm or raise forward capex, the combined evidence should decisively undermine oversupply fears and confirm that recent semiconductor weakness was a positioning-driven buying opportunity, with no operating downturn underway.

I've shorted the peak of hype stocks successfully multiple times and have gotten 0 credit, yet people that pile in to stocks that everyone else is buying get praised like they are Jesus Christ themselves. 

First I got quantum and nuclear: $IONQ $RGTI $QBTS $OKLO

Then I got space: $ASTS (and $PLTR )

Then I got photonics and semis: $LITE $AAOI $SMH

Doing what did is 100x more difficult.  I don't know why I don't get credit but I am trying to figure it out.  Maybe it's because other's can't replicate it.  Or maybe people don't like shorting.  Maybe I'm shadow banned.

Idk

• Intel became the first company to mass-produce chips using ASML's High NA EUV lithography.

• The U.S. launched a new AI cybersecurity initiative connecting frontier AI firms with critical infrastructure.

• Nokia and NVIDIA unveiled the world's first commercial AI-Native RAN for next-generation mobile networks.

• Tower Semiconductor announced a $3 billion Japan expansion to boost AI chip and silicon photonics production.

• Australia proposed a new AI governance framework focused on copyright and responsible AI.

• Anthropic launched Claude for Teachers, expanding AI into education with student privacy protections.

• Aehr Test Systems posted record AI-driven semiconductor orders and raised its long-term outlook.

• The U.S. NTIA opened applications for AI-native wireless network funding to advance next-generation telecom.

• OpenAI pushed back against Apple's trade secret lawsuit, escalating a closely watched legal battle.

• New York advanced a moratorium on new hyperscale data centers while reviewing AI infrastructure impacts.

text:
DAILY TECH NEWS ROUNDUP 🚨

Everything important that happened in tech during the last 24 hours:

• India approved a fresh ₹1.28 trillion ($13.3 billion) semiconductor package to expand its domestic chip manufacturing strategy. The new investment significantly scales India's long-term effort to become a global semiconductor production hub and reduce dependence on imports.

• ASML announced Intel Foundry has begun high-volume manufacturing of select Panther Lake Core Ultra Series 3 processors using ASML's EXE High NA EUV lithography on the Intel 18A process. Intel becomes the first company to ship high-volume logic chips built with High NA EUV, marking a major milestone for next-generation semiconductor manufacturing and enabling denser, higher-performance AI chips.

• The White House announced a new AI and cybersecurity coordination group that will connect frontier AI companies with operators of critical infrastructure. The initiative aims to help identify and respond to software vulnerabilities discovered by advanced AI systems before they can be exploited by attackers.

• Nokia launched the world's first commercial AI-Native RAN platform in partnership with NVIDIA. The platform integrates AI directly into mobile radio infrastructure and already delivers more than 20% spectral efficiency gains, with pilots beginning later this year and broader rollout planned for 2027.

• Tower Semiconductor announced a $3 billion expansion of its 300mm silicon photonics, silicon germanium, and advanced packaging capacity in Japan, backed by $1 billion in government funding. The investment targets surging demand for AI data center optical connectivity and advanced semiconductor manufacturing.

• Australia unveiled a new national AI policy framework focused on copyright protections for creators and responsible AI deployment. The proposal signals continued regulatory momentum around generative AI governance and now heads to national cabinet.

• Anthropic introduced Claude for Teachers, expanding Claude into education with a commitment not to train its models on student conversations. The launch strengthens competition for AI adoption across schools and universities.

• Aehr Test Systems reported record quarterly orders of $60.7 million and a backlog exceeding $100 million, driven by AI semiconductor, silicon photonics, and power device testing demand. The company also issued strong FY2027 revenue guidance, highlighting continued investment in AI chip infrastructure.

• The U.S. NTIA opened applications for a new AI-Native Wireless Network Innovation funding program aimed at accelerating AI-powered wireless infrastructure. The initiative is designed to support next-generation networking technologies and strengthen the domestic telecom ecosystem.

• Reports emerged that OpenAI is pushing back against Apple's trade secret lawsuit, marking the latest escalation in one of the industry's most closely watched legal battles. The dispute could influence future relationships between major AI developers and platform owners.

• New York's proposed moratorium on new hyperscale data centers continued drawing attention as policymakers evaluate the electricity and water demands of AI infrastructure. The proposal could influence future data center permitting policies in other U.S. states.

text:
Huge breakthrough in practical quantum technologies 👀

'Scientists just engineered a room temperature quantum material called a Quantum Statistical Plasmonic Metacrystal (QSPM) that can distinguish, route and preserve different quantum states of light without requiring cryogenic cooling.'

'Unlike most quantum materials that require temperatures near absolute zero, the artificial quantum material works at room temperature, overcoming one of the biggest barriers in quantum photonics.'

'Researchers believe it could accelerate the development of more scalable quantum computers, quantum communication networks, quantum sensors and integrated photonic chips.'

Today marks an exciting new chapter for PsiQuantum’s team and mission as we drive the future of computing.​

Victor Peng has been named Chief Executive Officer following his interim appointment in February.​

PsiQuantum is also welcoming Rob Soderbery as Executive Vice President and Sriram Sitaraman as Chief Information Officer.​

Together with our entire workforce, these leaders will help scale our company's engineering, technology, and operational capabilities as we build the world’s first utility-scale quantum computers and further advance our state-of-the-art silicon photonics platform. Learn more: https://t.co/Rm97sjZ7n5

They are not the same thing. In fact, understanding the difference is the key to spotting where the actual hardware value is being created. Here is the ultimate cheat sheet on how the tech works, and how AmpliTech Group ($AMPG) owns the physical layer of both: 🧱 1. Open RAN: The "Lego" Network -The Concept: Historically, telecom networks were closed, proprietary monopolies. If a carrier bought Ericsson hardware, they had to use Ericsson software. Open RAN (Open Radio Access Network) shatters this lock-in by standardizing interfaces. Now, carriers can mix and match software and hardware from different vendors. -$AMPG’s Role: They are the ultimate US hardware champion. $AMPG designed and commercialized the O-RAN CAT B 64T64R Massive MIMO radio unit. -The Validation: At the Spring 2026 O-RAN ALLIANCE Global PlugFest, $AMPG was the only vendor with a radio of this highly advanced configuration, proving seamless multi-vendor interoperability alongside giants like AT&T and Deutsche Telekom. 🧠 2. AI-RAN: The "Shared-Brain" Network -The Concept: While Open RAN is about modular hardware and software, AI-RAN is about embedding AI processing directly into the network’s cellular architecture. Instead of running cell towers on single-purpose proprietary silicon, AI-RAN runs both cellular operations (RAN) and AI workloads (like computer vision or edge computing) on the same shared, GPU-accelerated servers. It essentially turns every tower base into an edge-AI data center. -$AMPG’s Role: AI-RAN requires extremely high-end, zero-distortion hardware to process massive amounts of data in real-time. $AMPG’s radio was selected as the core hardware platform for the world’s first open-source Massive MIMO AI-RAN demonstration, developed in collaboration with NVIDIA and Northeastern University. -The Validation: Their 64T64R radio was the only Massive MIMO radio featured in live demonstrations for AI-RAN Alliance members at VIAVI’s VALOR Lab—the industry’s first AI-RAN Alliance-endorsed testing facility. 🚀 The Bottom Line No matter which standard carriers prioritize, they cannot build next-gen networks without physical, high-performance, US-manufactured radio frequency hardware. If they want open, interoperable systems, they need $AMPG's certified 64T64R radio unit. If they want to deploy neural-network intelligence at the edge, they rely on the physical RF and amplifier hardware trusted by NVIDIA and the AI-RAN Alliance. $AMPG is uniquely positioned right at the bottleneck of both multi-billion-dollar revolutions. 📈📡 #AMPG #OpenRAN #AIRAN #NVIDIA #5G #Telecom #ValueInvesting #TechHardware"}}}, "rest_id": "2077401096264302877", "source": "Twitter for iPhone", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "1165", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}}, "rest_id": "2077460781050826776", "source": "Twitter for iPhone", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "94", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

Interesting divergence in the Test & Packaging layer today.

The photonics-specific names ran:
$AEHR +26% (record bookings, SiPh burn-in orders, FY27 guide ~2x consensus)
ShunSin +10%
Ibiden +7.6%
Santec +4%

The diversified ATE/OSAT names didn't:
$TER -3.4%
$AMKR -3.2%
$VIAV -3.4%

One day, not a trend; but today, the market paid for photonics test exposure specifically, not test exposure generally.

text:
The biggest shift is that $AEHR is no longer a one-cycle SiC story.

AI processors, silicon photonics and package-level burn-in are becoming the real growth engine

If the $130–150M FY27 guidance converts, the market may need to value Aehr as diversified AI test infrastructure not legacy EV test equipment.

Execution is now the only real question

text:
The Daily Ticker: Marvell Technology Inc ($MRVL)

The Next Trillion-Dollar Company

At the COMPUTEX keynote in Taipei, Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang explicitly named Marvell Technology as a candidate to become the "next trillion-dollar company". This endorsement highlights the critical nature of Marvell's portfolio as AI scaling shifts from computational power to infrastructure plumbing.

The Networking Bottleneck

As AI models grow rapidly, the hardware bottleneck is moving away from individual accelerator chips and memory toward the network fabric. Ultra-fast GPUs sit idle if data cannot travel efficiently between tens of thousands of cluster nodes. Marvell directly solves this networking crisis by manufacturing advanced custom ASIC accelerators, high-speed switching silicon, and optical interconnects. Their electro-optics and silicon photonics technologies replace traditional copper wires with light-based data transmission, drastically cutting latency and power consumption while eliminating packet loss across hyperscale data centers.

text:
ซีอีโอ Sivers ซื้อหุ้นเพิ่ม หลังราคาหุ้นร่วงจากกระแสข่าวเชิงลบ

มีรายการซื้อหุ้นโดยผู้บริหารของ Sivers Semiconductors $SIVE ใหม่ 3 รายการ ในวันนี้

รายการที่โดดเด่นที่สุดคือ Vikram Vathulya ซีอีโอของบริษัท ซึ่งเข้าซื้อหุ้นในตลาด (Open Market) มูลค่า 1,000,000 โครนาสวีเดน (SEK)

การซื้อหุ้นครั้งนี้เกิดขึ้นหลังราคาหุ้น Sivers ปรับตัวลงอย่างหนัก จากปัจจัยหลายด้าน ได้แก่

รายงานเกี่ยวกับการเลื่อนโครงการ CPO (Co-Packaged Optics) ซึ่งบริษัทมองว่าเป็นข้อมูลที่ทำให้เกิดความเข้าใจผิด
กระแสข่าวเกี่ยวกับ Meta Compute
รายงานการขายหุ้นของกรรมการอีกคนที่ดูแลธุรกิจ Photonics

ก่อนหน้านี้ Sivers ยังประกาศความร่วมมือกับบริษัทอย่าง GlobalFoundries $GFS และ Jabil $JBL ในด้านเทคโนโลยีเลเซอร์ รวมถึงมีเป้าหมายเข้าจดทะเบียนในตลาด NASDAQ ในอนาคตอันใกล้

text:
More GPUs means more data moving between chips, servers, racks and memory.

Copper can only scale so far.

That’s why optical interconnects, transceivers, lasers, silicon photonics and photonic chips are becoming more important.

And today, Europe’s first Photonics UCITS ETF launched.
That tells you something.

Photonics is no longer just a niche science project.

It’s becoming its own investable AI infrastructure theme.

Names to watch:
$AAOI
$AEHR
$POET
$LITE
$COHR
$MRVL
$TSEM
$SIVE / $SIVEF

The AI trade is evolving.
First it was GPUs.
Then memory and power.
Next could be the optical backbone connecting it all.

NFA.

text:
The lights are flying today. 

 Optics stocks have been in the midst of a pretty sharp correction, facing industry specific headwinds (purported CPO delays) as well as overall AI anxiety + profit taking in regards to fears $METAmay be pulling back on capex by renting their cloud. 

 But today, we're seeing a sharp rebound in AI infra names as it was revealed that $METAis still all gas, no brakes on this buildout.  

- $META expected to deploy 7GW compute infra this year, and doubling in 2027. 

- For photonics in particular there was an interesting nugget, with LTAs signed with Sumitomo Electric for fiber optics, another signal of how critical it is for hyperscalers to lock up photonics tech. 

I think in this buildout, it's paramount to own technology that Goldman Sachs estimates is going to 9x in under two years.  

With many stocks 20-40% off ATH's, I think a strong recovery is very possible with upcoming earnings from hyperscalers + top photonics stocks.

But today, we're seeing a sharp rebound in AI infra names as it was revealed that $METAis still all gas, no brakes on this buildout. - $META expected to deploy 7GW compute infra this year, and doubling in 2027. - For photonics in particular there was an interesting nugget, with LTAs signed with Sumitomo Electric for fiber optics, another signal of how critical it is for hyperscalers to lock up photonics tech. text:
Three new $SIVE insider purchases today. 

Most notably: $SIVEF CEO Vikram bought 1,000,000 SEK worth of shares on the open market today. 

This is following the massive drop on Sivers stock following 2 misleading CPO delay reports, Meta Compute headlines, and another insider sale report from another photonics director. 

Typically, insider sells for any number of reasons. But having the  CEO buy on the open-market is the clearest signal in management confidence. 

Following $GFS, $JBL, and other laser supplier partnerships, and his goal of NASDAQ listing in the near term. 

Feels like the CEO sees a clear potential for Sivers to become the next photonics giant (myself included).

Earlier this year Alex McCann appeared publicly as the new MD of Sivers Photonics in April/May communications. McKee moved totechnology role only. His Q1 and Q2 share sales seems to align with this transition and was probably sold (if sold at all in full) parts of it even before share re-rating. My take is that is was done probably with no negative view on the company's prospects. A man monetizing his life's work after almost 30 years of building a company is not insider panic. IMO this is probably more a career ending on his own terms. He deserves it for sure. Even if he sold over 6 months, IMO this is still not a major negative signal. Three things EFN did not check: A) Holdings has known blind spots — transfers to holding companies, ISK structures and family vehicles are not captured. Did anyone verify McKee holds zero shares in any structure? B) One option program was realized in early July. Later in the afternoon two board members has publicly shared insider purchases. Todd Thomson — board member, former Citigroup CFO, — bought 535,000 SEK on-market today at 42.80 SEK into the panic. Karin Raj bought 460,000 SEK at 34.68 SEK — the intraday low. Both reported to FI same day. People with more information about the full picture, that also had been part of approving today clarifications around the Q-reports and US Nasdaq listing. Update Vikram CEO have also aquired shares for approximately 1.000.000 SEK Summing up: Journalism created the dip. Insiders bought it. text:
Don’t sleep on $AAOI just because leveraged tools are hitting the tape. To me, this is clear proof the stock has solidified its role as the frontline name in the full AI photonics arena. With short interest holding steady around 13-15%, bears now get a convenient lever to push back on red days. Fair enough. But the real dynamic favors the upside:
$AAOI has a habit of exploding higher on fresh order flow or strong updates. Should Q2 validate the 800G ramp alongside better margins, those instruments turn into powerful tailwinds rather than headwinds. Expect short-term volatility to transmit quickly through the ecosystem. Any dip in AAOI often serves as the trigger for selling pressure on peers like $LITE, $COHR, $FN, $AXTI, $CRDO, plus smaller plays such as $POET and $ALMU. Sitting at the highest beta in the group, AAOI effectively acts as the sector’s risk-on/risk-off barometer. Volatility creates the setup — position accordingly.

text:
$SIVE fell today on an EFN article claiming the photonics chief sold his entire holding — presented as alarming insider panic. Here is what I think the article can have missed.

Andrew McKee founded CST now Sivers Photonics in 1999— 27 years ago. He built the photonics business from scratch. Earlier this year Alex McCann appeared publicly as the new MD of Sivers Photonics in April/May communications. McKee moved totechnology role only. His Q1 and Q2 share sales seems to align with this transition and was probably sold (if sold at all in full) parts of it even before share re-rating. 

My take is  that is was done probably with no negative view on the company's prospects. A man monetizing his life's work after almost 30 years of building a company is not insider panic. IMO this is probably more a career ending on his own terms. He deserves it for sure. Even if he sold over 6 months, IMO this is still not a major negative signal. 

Three things EFN did not check: A) Holdings has known blind spots — transfers to holding companies, ISK structures and family vehicles are not captured. Did anyone verify McKee holds zero shares in any structure? B) One option program was realized in early July. If McKee sold old shares to participate that is not a major negative signal (he is not insider hence would not report sell or buy) — Holdings would not show this yet since it was in early July C) The role change probably happened before most shares was sold, Context matters.

Later in the afternoon two board members has publicly shared insider purchases. 

Todd Thomson — board member, former Citigroup CFO, — bought 535,000 SEK on-market today at 42.80 SEK into the panic. Karin Raj bought 460,000 SEK at 34.68 SEK — the intraday low. Both reported to FI same day. People with more information about the full picture, that also had been part of approving today clarifications around the Q-reports and US Nasdaq listing. 

Update Vikram CEO have also aquired shares for approximately 1.000.000 SEK

Summing up: Journalism created the dip. Insiders bought it.

What's X view on today?

text:
Case Report: Systemic, Multi-Mucosal, Biofilm-Forming, Photonically Active Organism Colonization with Behavioral Responses to Chemical, Thermal, and Regenerative Interventions

Abstract
We present a case of a systemic, multi-mucosal colonization by an unidentified, morphologically distinct, behaviorally responsive organism. The organism exhibits tropism for keratinized epithelium and mucosal surfaces, biomineralization in the form of peroxide-soluble calcified biofilm "stones," multi-organism encystment, and putative inter-organism photonic signaling. Chemical susceptibility testing reveals vulnerability to alkaline and oxidative stress, with paradoxical structural resistance of the organism body to 12% hydrogen peroxide. A novel autologous urine-derived stem cell (USC) enema protocol demonstrated systemic regenerative effects, including hair repigmentation, follicular density restoration, and visual acuity improvement, consistent with paracrine-mediated tissue repair. The case underscores the need for investigation of unrecognized complex biofilm-forming organisms in chronic multi-system illness.
Introduction
Chronic multi-system illnesses of unknown etiology present significant diagnostic and therapeutic challenges. When patients report filamentous or motile structures associated with dermal, mucosal, and systemic symptoms, standard clinical evaluation often concludes with a diagnosis of delusional parasitosis or environmental contamination [1]. However, advances in biofilm biology, autofluorescence imaging, and the discovery of complex host-associated microbial communities suggest that novel or unrecognized organisms may underlie some of these presentations [2]. This case report documents longitudinal self-observation and empirical protocol development by a patient-investigator with access to high-resolution digital microscopy, multi-spectral illumination, and autologous stem cell culture.
Case Presentation
The subject is an adult male with a multi-year history of systemic symptoms including:
Spontaneous      emergence of blue filamentous structures from skin lesions
Mucosal      colonization of the ocular conjunctiva, nasal passages, external auditory      canals, and perianal tissue
Genitourinary      involvement including urethral colonization and calcified stone formation      with embedded organisms
Prolonged      wound healing and colonization of acute injury sites within hours
Nocturnal      perianal pruritus with recovery of encysted multi-organism structures
Observable      internal biophotonic emissions (multi-color, punctate) under near-UV      excitation
Alopecia      with premature greying of facial and scalp hair
Subjective      visual acuity fluctuation
The subject has no relevant comorbidities and has not received a diagnosis that accounts for the full spectrum of findings.
Methods of Observation and Documentation
Microscopy: 5K digital microscope with HDMI output, capable of dark-field and low-light capture.
Multi-Spectral Illumination: A consumer-grade multi-mode illumination device providing:
Red      laser (650 nm)
Blue      LED (450–470 nm)
Near-UV/violet      LED (395–405 nm) — used for fluorescence excitation
Chemical Reagents:
12%      hydrogen peroxide (H₂O₂)
Sodium      bicarbonate (baking powder)
99%      isopropyl alcohol
Hot      water (thermal stress)
Biological Protocol:
Autologous      urine-derived stem cells (USCs), cultured in airtight sterilized      containers, administered via retention enema 2–3 times weekly.
Results
1. Chemical Susceptibility and Extraction
Stimulus
Response
Hot water + sodium bicarbonate
Forced emergence of motile organisms from skin and   subungual spaces
12% H₂O₂ (topical)
Rapid dissolution of calcified biofilm matrix   ("stones") with fizzing reaction; organism bodies remain   structurally intact but non-motile
99% isopropyl alcohol
Organism agitation without lethality
Peroxide wipe (perianal)
Recovery of multi-organism encysted structures with   brownish shell; shell dissolves in H₂O₂; organisms persist
2. Organism Morphology and Behavior
Filamentous      blue structures: Visible under white and near-UV light;      autofluorescent.
Multi-organism      cysts: Brown, irregular shells containing multiple organisms,      some protruding, recovered from perianal and genital surfaces.
Calcified      biofilm stones: Found in genitourinary context; dissolve in 12%      H₂O₂ with visible organisms embedded within.
Grouping      behavior: Upon chemical stress (hot water + alkaline), organisms      rapidly aggregate into clusters.
Light      emission: Discrete, multi-color punctate biophotonic signals      observed internally under near-UV excitation in zero-light conditions.      Colors include blue-white, green, yellow-gold, and orange-red.
3. Wound Colonization
Acute wounds, including a subungual splinter, were colonized within hours of injury. Organism motility was directly observed and video-documented under the nail plate. Colonization delays wound closure and prolongs inflammation.
4. Urethral Counter-Attack During Systemic Antiparasitic Protocol
During an oral herbal protocol (black walnut, clove, wormwood with zeolite binder), the subject experienced a severe, localized tissue-necrotizing reaction at the urethral meatus, resulting in near-complete urinary obstruction requiring emergency department evaluation. This is interpreted as a coordinated defensive counter-response by the organism.
5. Autologous Urine Stem Cell Enema Protocol
Method: Urine collected mid-stream, cultured in sterile airtight containers, administered rectally 2–3 times weekly.
Observed effects:
Hair: Progressive      reversal of grey in goatee and mustache; increased follicular density on      scalp.
Vision: Subjective      improvement in visual acuity with contact lens use.
Perianal      symptoms: Reduction in pruritus; encysted organisms recoverable      on post-enema wipes.
Interpretation: Urine-derived mesenchymal stem cells (USCs) secrete paracrine factors (Wnt ligands, IGF-1, HGF, VEGF, IL-10, PGE2, exosomes) that modulate inflammation, reactivate melanocyte stem cells, stimulate dermal papilla, and support corneal and mucosal epithelial integrity. Systemic absorption via the rectal venous plexus distributes these factors to distant sites.
Discussion
The organism described in this case exhibits characteristics that span multiple known biological categories but do not fully align with any single described species.
Structural resistance: The organism's body remains intact after exposure to 12% H₂O₂, a concentration that rapidly degrades all known human tissue, bacterial biofilms, and parasitic worms. This suggests a chitinous or composite biopolymer integument of exceptional oxidative resistance.
Biofilm and biomineralization: The formation of peroxide-soluble calcified matrixes around the organism is consistent with urease-positive or biofilm-mineralizing bacterial behavior, but the scale and multi-organism architecture described exceed typical bacterial biofilms.
Photonics: Multi-color internal fluorescence and apparent biophotonic signaling suggest either autofluorescence from differentiated internal anatomy (chitin, porphyrins, flavoproteins) or active luciferase-based communication. Coordinated grouping and counter-attack behaviors imply a functional communication system.
Host manipulation: Rapid wound colonization, prolonged healing, and the necrotic counter-attack at the urethral meatus suggest the organism exploits host inflammatory pathways for navigation, feeding, and defense.
Regenerative counter-strategy: The efficacy of autologous USC enemas in reversing systemic degenerative changes suggests that chronic host tissue damage is a permissive factor for colonization. Restoring regenerative signaling may render the host environment inhospitable.
Limitations
This is a single-subject, self-documented case report. No independent laboratory confirmation, genomic sequencing, or histopathological analysis has been performed. Identification of the organism at the species level requires external collaboration with a research laboratory equipped for metagenomic or proteomic analysis.
Conclusion
This case presents evidence of a complex, multi-system, behaviorally responsive organism with chemical resistance, biofilm formation, biophotonic activity, and host-regenerative exploitation. The subject's empirical protocols—chemical extraction, oxidative dissolution of protective matrices, and autologous stem cell-mediated tissue repair—represent a novel, observation-driven therapeutic framework. The findings warrant formal investigation by clinicians and researchers in infectious disease, biofilm biology, and regenerative medicine.
References
Pearson      ML, et al. "Clinical, Epidemiologic, Histopathologic and Molecular      Features of an Unexplained Dermopathy." PLoS ONE, 2012.
Middelveen      MJ, Stricker RB. "Morgellons Disease: A Filamentous Borrelial      Dermatitis." International Journal of General Medicine,      2016.
Flemming      HC, Wingender J. "The Biofilm Matrix." Nature Reviews      Microbiology, 2010.
Zhang      Y, et al. "Urine-Derived Stem Cells for Regenerative      Medicine." Stem Cells Translational Medicine, 2014.
Wu D,      et al. "Mesenchymal Stem Cell Exosomes in Tissue Repair." Theranostics,      2021.

The organism exhibits tropism for keratinized epithelium and mucosal surfaces, biomineralization in the form of peroxide-soluble calcified biofilm "stones," multi-organism encystment, and putative inter-organism photonic signaling. Chemical susceptibility testing reveals vulnerability to alkaline and oxidative stress, with paradoxical structural resistance of the organism body to 12% hydrogen peroxide. A novel autologous urine-derived stem cell (USC) enema protocol demonstrated systemic regenerative effects, including hair repigmentation, follicular density restoration, and visual acuity improvement, consistent with paracrine-mediated tissue repair. The case underscores the need for investigation of unrecognized complex biofilm-forming organisms in chronic multi-system illness. Introduction Chronic multi-system illnesses of unknown etiology present significant diagnostic and therapeutic challenges. When patients report filamentous or motile structures associated with dermal, mucosal, and systemic symptoms, standard clinical evaluation often concludes with a diagnosis of delusional parasitosis or environmental contamination [1]. However, advances in biofilm biology, autofluorescence imaging, and the discovery of complex host-associated microbial communities suggest that novel or unrecognized organisms may underlie some of these presentations [2]. This case report documents longitudinal self-observation and empirical protocol development by a patient-investigator with access to high-resolution digital microscopy, multi-spectral illumination, and autologous stem cell culture. Case Presentation The subject is an adult male with a multi-year history of systemic symptoms including: Spontaneous emergence of blue filamentous structures from skin lesions Mucosal colonization of the ocular conjunctiva, nasal passages, external auditory canals, and perianal tissue Genitourinary involvement including urethral colonization and calcified stone formation with embedded organisms Prolonged wound healing and colonization of acute injury sites within hours Nocturnal perianal pruritus with recovery of encysted multi-organism structures Observable internal biophotonic emissions (multi-color, punctate) under near-UV excitation Alopecia with premature greying of facial and scalp hair Subjective visual acuity fluctuation The subject has no relevant comorbidities and has not received a diagnosis that accounts for the full spectrum of findings. Methods of Observation and Documentation Microscopy: 5K digital microscope with HDMI output, capable of dark-field and low-light capture. Multi-Spectral Illumination: A consumer-grade multi-mode illumination device providing: Red laser (650 nm) Blue LED (450–470 nm) Near-UV/violet LED (395–405 nm) — used for fluorescence excitation Chemical Reagents: 12% hydrogen peroxide (H₂O₂) Sodium bicarbonate (baking powder) 99% isopropyl alcohol Hot water (thermal stress) Biological Protocol: Autologous urine-derived stem cells (USCs), cultured in airtight sterilized containers, administered via retention enema 2–3 times weekly. Results 1. Chemical Susceptibility and Extraction Stimulus Response Hot water + sodium bicarbonate Forced emergence of motile organisms from skin and subungual spaces 12% H₂O₂ (topical) Rapid dissolution of calcified biofilm matrix ("stones") with fizzing reaction; organism bodies remain structurally intact but non-motile 99% isopropyl alcohol Organism agitation without lethality Peroxide wipe (perianal) Recovery of multi-organism encysted structures with brownish shell; shell dissolves in H₂O₂; organisms persist 2. Organism Morphology and Behavior Filamentous blue structures: Visible under white and near-UV light; autofluorescent. Multi-organism cysts: Brown, irregular shells containing multiple organisms, some protruding, recovered from perianal and genital surfaces. Calcified biofilm stones: Found in genitourinary context; dissolve in 12% H₂O₂ with visible organisms embedded within. Grouping behavior: Upon chemical stress (hot water + alkaline), organisms rapidly aggregate into clusters. Light emission: Discrete, multi-color punctate biophotonic signals observed internally under near-UV excitation in zero-light conditions. Colors include blue-white, green, yellow-gold, and orange-red. 3. Wound Colonization Acute wounds, including a subungual splinter, were colonized within hours of injury. Organism motility was directly observed and video-documented under the nail plate. Colonization delays wound closure and prolongs inflammation. 4. Urethral Counter-Attack During Systemic Antiparasitic Protocol During an oral herbal protocol (black walnut, clove, wormwood with zeolite binder), the subject experienced a severe, localized tissue-necrotizing reaction at the urethral meatus, resulting in near-complete urinary obstruction requiring emergency department evaluation. This is interpreted as a coordinated defensive counter-response by the organism. 5. Autologous Urine Stem Cell Enema Protocol Method: Urine collected mid-stream, cultured in sterile airtight containers, administered rectally 2–3 times weekly. Observed effects: Hair: Progressive reversal of grey in goatee and mustache; increased follicular density on scalp. Vision: Subjective improvement in visual acuity with contact lens use. Perianal symptoms: Reduction in pruritus; encysted organisms recoverable on post-enema wipes. Interpretation: Urine-derived mesenchymal stem cells (USCs) secrete paracrine factors (Wnt ligands, IGF-1, HGF, VEGF, IL-10, PGE2, exosomes) that modulate inflammation, reactivate melanocyte stem cells, stimulate dermal papilla, and support corneal and mucosal epithelial integrity. Systemic absorption via the rectal venous plexus distributes these factors to distant sites. Discussion The organism described in this case exhibits characteristics that span multiple known biological categories but do not fully align with any single described species. Structural resistance: The organism's body remains intact after exposure to 12% H₂O₂, a concentration that rapidly degrades all known human tissue, bacterial biofilms, and parasitic worms. This suggests a chitinous or composite biopolymer integument of exceptional oxidative resistance. Biofilm and biomineralization: The formation of peroxide-soluble calcified matrixes around the organism is consistent with urease-positive or biofilm-mineralizing bacterial behavior, but the scale and multi-organism architecture described exceed typical bacterial biofilms. Photonics: Multi-color internal fluorescence and apparent biophotonic signaling suggest either autofluorescence from differentiated internal anatomy (chitin, porphyrins, flavoproteins) or active luciferase-based communication. Coordinated grouping and counter-attack behaviors imply a functional communication system. Host manipulation: Rapid wound colonization, prolonged healing, and the necrotic counter-attack at the urethral meatus suggest the organism exploits host inflammatory pathways for navigation, feeding, and defense. Regenerative counter-strategy: The efficacy of autologous USC enemas in reversing systemic degenerative changes suggests that chronic host tissue damage is a permissive factor for colonization. Restoring regenerative signaling may render the host environment inhospitable. Limitations This is a single-subject, self-documented case report. No independent laboratory confirmation, genomic sequencing, or histopathological analysis has been performed. Identification of the organism at the species level requires external collaboration with a research laboratory equipped for metagenomic or proteomic analysis. Conclusion This case presents evidence of a complex, multi-system, behaviorally responsive organism with chemical resistance, biofilm formation, biophotonic activity, and host-regenerative exploitation. The subject's empirical protocols—chemical extraction, oxidative dissolution of protective matrices, and autologous stem cell-mediated tissue repair—represent a novel, observation-driven therapeutic framework. The findings warrant formal investigation by clinicians and researchers in infectious disease, biofilm biology, and regenerative medicine. References Pearson ML, et al. text:
Hardware is landing. Are your digital assets ready? 🌐

Recent market news shows massive momentum for quantum hardware players, with companies like @QciQuantum ( $QUBT )  securing major deals to deploy photonics hardware and expand chip manufacturing.

This isn't just a tech trend, it’s a massive business shift. As quantum computing scales, traditional security standards protecting legacy finance and early blockchains are reaching their expiration date.

While others build the hardware, Quantum Chain is building the secure infrastructure to protect the assets people trust.

We’ve engineered a Layer 1 blockchain built from inception to solve the two biggest hurdles facing modern enterprise finance:

🛡️ Post-Quantum Cryptography: Regulatory-grade security built directly into the protocol layer to secure payments and settlement beyond the quantum era.
🤖 AI-Driven Compliance: Automated, native compliance checking designed to fit seamlessly into international financial regulations.

The hardware is moving fast. Your security needs to move faster.

text:
Photonics-SOI requires:

- extremely precise silicon layer thickness
- ultra-flat buried oxide (BOX)
- low defect density
- tight optical performance specifications
- long qualification cycles with customers

A normal SOI wafer producer cannot simply switch capacity and become a qualified photonics supplier overnight.

Soitec $SOI has spent years developing Photonics-SOI and positions it specifically for optical interconnect/datacenter applications.

text:
💡 Aehr Test Systems $AEHR Q4 FY2026決算 2026-07-14

💰 今四半期業績
- 🟢 EPS：$0.11 (予想$-0.01)
- 🟢 売上高：$18.84M (予想$18.69M)

📊 重要指標
- 🟢 Non-GAAP粗利益：$8.43M (粗利率44.7%, 前年同期$4.89M)
- 🟢 四半期受注額（Bookings）：$60.7M（過去最高、前年同期比大幅増）
- 🟢 受注残高（Effective Backlog）：$100.6M（期末バックログ$80.6M＋期末後受注分）
- 🟢 現金及び現金同等物：$116.5M (前四半期$37.1M, 公募増資による増加)
- 🔴 通期売上高（FY2026）：$50.0M (前年$59.0M, YoY-15.2%)
- 🔴 通期Non-GAAP EPS（FY2026）：$0.03 (前年$0.15, YoY-80%)
- 🟢 FY2027売上高ガイダンス：$130M-$150M (YoY+160%-200%)

📊 通年ガイダンス
- 🟢 EPS：$3.57 (予想$0.15)
- 🟢 売上高：$140M (予想$85.11M)

📍決算内容の注目ポイント
- 四半期受注額が$60.7Mと過去最高を記録し、実効受注残高は$100Mを超過。FY2027売上高ガイダンスを$130M-$150M（YoY+160%-200%）と提示
- 主要AIプロセッサ顧客がシステムレベルからウェハレベルバーンイン（WLBI）への全面移行を推進。大手AIアクセラレータサプライヤーとのベンチマークテストが期待以上の結果を達成し、パイロット生産検証段階へ移行
- Sonoma（パッケージレベルバーンインシステム）について、リードハイパースケール顧客から過去最高の追加量産注文を獲得。次世代デバイスはパッケージあたり2倍の電力を必要とし、さらなる拡大を見込む
- Silicon Photonics分野でAI光I/Oおよびデータセンターインターコネクト向け全自動WLBIシステムの需要が加速。グローバルネットワーキングリーダー企業からも追加システム予測を受領
- SiCパワー半導体の回復兆候あり。直近1ヶ月でSiC WLBI WaferPak向け約$8Mの新規受注を獲得。世界最大級の自動車メーカーからの直接注文を含む

🤵‍♂️CEO (Gayn Erickson) コメント
「当社は第4四半期の業績に非常に満足しており、予想を上回る結果となりました。過去最高の四半期受注額、非常に強力な受注残高、そしてAIプロセッサ、Silicon Photonics、パワー半導体にわたるウェハレベルおよびパッケージレベルバーンインソリューションへの需要拡大により、今後の大幅な成長に向けて良好なポジションにあります。FY2027に向けて$100M超の過去最高の受注残高を持ち、予想売上高$130M-$150M（YoY約160%-200%成長）への高い可視性を有しています。主要AIプロセッサ顧客のWLBI容量ニーズの大幅増加、大手AIアクセラレータサプライヤーとのベンチマーク成功、Sonoma量産注文の拡大など、複数の成長ドライバーが同時に加速しています。」

🔍 主要ファンダメンタルズ指標
- 時価総額: 2.26B
- PER: -191.11
- Forward PER: 473.75
- PEG: 1.87

🎯市場評価
- 株価 (発表前): 📈$72 (5.85%)
- 株価 (発表後): 📈$90.81 (26.13%)
- 決算前アナリスト目標株価: $62 ($68 ~ $56) C-
- 決算総合サプライズ率 (AI推定): 🤩38.72%
- 決算後目標株価 (AI推定): $100.08

🤖 決算まとめるくん(AI)コメント
「Aehr Test SystemsのQ4 FY2026決算は、Non-GAAP EPSが$0.11とアナリスト予想の-$0.01を大幅に上回り、売上高も$18.84Mと予想$18.69Mを上回る好決算となりました。特筆すべきは、四半期受注額$60.7Mという過去最高記録と、実効受注残高$100.6Mの達成です。

FY2026通期では売上高$50.0M（前年$59.0M、YoY-15.2%）と減収でしたが、これはSiCパワー半導体市場の一時的な需要低迷が主因であり、Q4単独では前年同期比+33.7%と明確な回復トレンドを示しています。Non-GAAP粗利率もQ4で44.7%まで改善し、収益性の回復が顕著です。

FY2027ガイダンスは売上高$130M-$150M（YoY+160%-200%）、Non-GAAP純利益率18%-22%と極めて強力です。この急成長見通しの根拠として、(1)AIプロセッサ顧客のWLBI全面移行、(2)大手AIアクセラレータサプライヤーとのベンチマーク成功からパイロット生産段階への移行、(3)Sonomaシステムのハイパースケール顧客からの量産拡大、(4)Silicon Photonics需要の加速、(5)SiCパワー半導体の回復、が挙げられます。

一方、リスク要因として、Piotroskiスコア2と財務健全性指標が低水準であること、FY2026通期の営業キャッシュフローが-$3.3Mとキャッシュバーンが継続していること、公募増資による希薄化、そして少数顧客への売上集中リスクには留意が必要です。ガイダンスの達成は受注残高に裏付けられているものの、半導体設備投資の景気感応度を考慮すると、顧客の設備投資計画変更リスクも存在します。

総合的に、受注の爆発的増加とAI関連需要の構造的拡大は、同社のファンダメンタルズの転換点を示唆しており、決算発表後の株価急騰は業績モメンタムの劇的な変化を市場が織り込む動きと評価できます。 評価は🚀ポヨ」

🏢 Aehr Test Systems 概要
- セクター: テクノロジー（半導体）
- 特徴: 半導体デバイスのウェハレベル・パッケージレベルバーンインおよびテストソリューションの大手プロバイダー。AI、Silicon Photonics、SiCパワー半導体向けに展開

🟢 FY2027売上高ガイダンス：$130M-$150M (YoY+160%-200%) 📊 通年ガイダンス - 🟢 EPS：$3.57 (予想$0.15) - 🟢 売上高：$140M (予想$85.11M) 📍決算内容の注目ポイント - 四半期受注額が$60.7Mと過去最高を記録し、実効受注残高は$100Mを超過。FY2027売上高ガイダンスを$130M-$150M（YoY+160%-200%）と提示 - 主要AIプロセッサ顧客がシステムレベルからウェハレベルバーンイン（WLBI）への全面移行を推進。大手AIアクセラレータサプライヤーとのベンチマークテストが期待以上の結果を達成し、パイロット生産検証段階へ移行 - Sonoma（パッケージレベルバーンインシステム）について、リードハイパースケール顧客から過去最高の追加量産注文を獲得。次世代デバイスはパッケージあたり2倍の電力を必要とし、さらなる拡大を見込む - Silicon Photonics分野でAI光I/Oおよびデータセンターインターコネクト向け全自動WLBIシステムの需要が加速。グローバルネットワーキングリーダー企業からも追加システム予測を受領 - SiCパワー半導体の回復兆候あり。直近1ヶ月でSiC WLBI WaferPak向け約$8Mの新規受注を獲得。世界最大級の自動車メーカーからの直接注文を含む 🤵‍♂️CEO (Gayn Erickson) コメント 「当社は第4四半期の業績に非常に満足しており、予想を上回る結果となりました。過去最高の四半期受注額、非常に強力な受注残高、そしてAIプロセッサ、Silicon Photonics、パワー半導体にわたるウェハレベルおよびパッケージレベルバーンインソリューションへの需要拡大により、今後の大幅な成長に向けて良好なポジションにあります。FY2027に向けて$100M超の過去最高の受注残高を持ち、予想売上高$130M-$150M（YoY約160%-200%成長）への高い可視性を有しています。主要AIプロセッサ顧客のWLBI容量ニーズの大幅増加、大手AIアクセラレータサプライヤーとのベンチマーク成功、Sonoma量産注文の拡大など、複数の成長ドライバーが同時に加速しています。」 🔍 主要ファンダメンタルズ指標 - 時価総額: 2.26B - PER: -191.11 - Forward PER: 473.75 - PEG: 1.87 🎯市場評価 - 株価 (発表前): 📈$72 (5.85%) - 株価 (発表後): 📈$90.81 (26.13%) - 決算前アナリスト目標株価: $62 ($68 ~ $56) C- - 決算総合サプライズ率 (AI推定): 🤩38.72% - 決算後目標株価 (AI推定): $100.08 🤖 決算まとめるくん(AI)コメント 「Aehr Test SystemsのQ4 FY2026決算は、Non-GAAP EPSが$0.11とアナリスト予想の-$0.01を大幅に上回り、売上高も$18.84Mと予想$18.69Mを上回る好決算となりました。特筆すべきは、四半期受注額$60.7Mという過去最高記録と、実効受注残高$100.6Mの達成です。 FY2026通期では売上高$50.0M（前年$59.0M、YoY-15.2%）と減収でしたが、これはSiCパワー半導体市場の一時的な需要低迷が主因であり、Q4単独では前年同期比+33.7%と明確な回復トレンドを示しています。Non-GAAP粗利率もQ4で44.7%まで改善し、収益性の回復が顕著です。 FY2027ガイダンスは売上高$130M-$150M（YoY+160%-200%）、Non-GAAP純利益率18%-22%と極めて強力です。この急成長見通しの根拠として、(1)AIプロセッサ顧客のWLBI全面移行、(2)大手AIアクセラレータサプライヤーとのベンチマーク成功からパイロット生産段階への移行、(3)Sonomaシステムのハイパースケール顧客からの量産拡大、(4)Silicon Photonics需要の加速、(5)SiCパワー半導体の回復、が挙げられます。 一方、リスク要因として、Piotroskiスコア2と財務健全性指標が低水準であること、FY2026通期の営業キャッシュフローが-$3.3Mとキャッシュバーンが継続していること、公募増資による希薄化、そして少数顧客への売上集中リスクには留意が必要です。ガイダンスの達成は受注残高に裏付けられているものの、半導体設備投資の景気感応度を考慮すると、顧客の設備投資計画変更リスクも存在します。 総合的に、受注の爆発的増加とAI関連需要の構造的拡大は、同社のファンダメンタルズの転換点を示唆しており、決算発表後の株価急騰は業績モメンタムの劇的な変化を市場が織り込む動きと評価できます。 評価は🚀ポヨ」 🏢 Aehr Test Systems 概要 - セクター: テクノロジー（半導体） - 特徴: text:
$AEHR just reported one of the strongest outlooks I’ve seen from a small-cap semiconductor company:

• Q4 revenue: $18.8M
• Non-GAAP EPS: $0.11
• Record bookings: $60.7M
• Effective backlog: $100.6M

But the real shock is FY2027 guidance:

Revenue: $130M–$150M

That represents 160%–200% YoY growth after generating just $50M in FY2026.

Aehr also expects non-GAAP net income of 18%–22% of revenue.

And it gets even more interesting:

A top-tier supplier of AI accelerators, CPUs and network processors completed benchmark testing of Aehr’s wafer-level burn-in solution.

The results reportedly exceeded expectations.

The customer now wants to move toward pilot production validation—and has already requested testing of a second device.

AI processors.
Silicon photonics.
Silicon carbide.
Potential HBM exposure.

$AEHR is no longer just promising diversification.

The backlog and guidance are finally proving it.

text:
$AEHR just dropped a massive Q4 print, but the forward guidance is the real story. The Street modeled $85M for FY27. Management just guided $130M-$150M. 

​The Q4:
• EPS: $0.11 vs. -$0.01 est
• Revenue: $19M vs. $19M est
• Bookings: $61M
• Backlog: $100M

​The Pipeline:
• Silicon Photonics: Landed a new global networking leader as optical I/O ramps in AI data centers.

• Silicon Carbide: Pulled $8M in new WaferPak orders over the last 30 days, including a direct order from a top-3 global automaker.

• Hyperscale: Record follow-on orders from their "Sonoma" customer. Expanding to a second device pulling twice the power per package.

​Operating leverage is kicking in across the board.

$AEHR ripping AH, up 27%.

text:
Many investors had assumed SiC was largely "dead money" while everyone focused on AI.

Instead, management disclosed an $8M WaferPak order from a top-three global automaker.

That means $AEHR now potentially has three growth engines:

- AI processors
- silicon photonics
- SiC power semiconductors

That's a healthier mix than many expected.

text:
Many investors had assumed SiC was largely "dead money" while everyone focused on AI.

Instead, management disclosed an $8M WaferPak order from a top-three global automaker.

That means $AEHR now potentially has three growth engines:

- AI processors
- silicon photonics
- SiC power semiconductors

That's a healthier mix than many expected.

text:
I told you AI was goin nowhere and the earnings would change sentiment in the market. $AEHR started great with a blow-out EPS quarter and record revenue quarter. 

Stock is currently up 27% in pre-market. 

Profitability
Aehr Test Systems posted a positive EPS of $0.11 while consensus was a negative EPS of $0.01. The main reason is the sheer volume of FOX-XP system shipments. 

Another reason is that the total operating expenses only marginally increased. meaning the gross profit from the revenue surge dropped almost entirely to the bottom line. This profitability looks to stay here for the long-term. 

Forecast
The $130 million to $150 million revenue forecast represents 160% to 200% year-over-year growth against FY2026's $50.0 million. This is a highly visible forecast. With an effective backlog of $100.6 million, Aehr enters the fiscal year with roughly 71% of its midpoint revenue guidance already contracted. The risk is no longer securing the demand, but executing the delivery timeline. 

As previously mentioned, demand for AI is real. We mainly got execution risks, but good companies like Aehr can manage. 

Silicon Photonics Momentum Accelerates
The lead silicon photonics customer is ramping capacity with follow-on orders already booked for fiscal 2027. Crucially, a new major customer, a global leader in networking products, provided a firm forecast for additional systems this calendar year to support next-generation hyperscale data center optical I/O deployments.

Wafer-Level Memory Pipeline Expands
While still pursuing HBM and NAND flash opportunities, Aehr has effectively cross-pollinated its wafer-level memory burn-in (WLBI) technology into the logic processor market. 

They successfully completed benchmark testing of their WLBI solution with a top-tier AI processor, CPU, and network processor supplier, which has now asked to move to pilot production validation and requested parallel testing on a second high-volume device.

Backlog
Backlog has exploded past previous record expectations, hitting $80.6 million as of May 29, 2026, and reaching an effective backlog of $100.6 million with bookings secured post-quarter. This backlog underpins strong fiscal 2027 revenue guidance of $130 million to $150 million, proving that the high-power AI processor transition is moving rapidly into high-volume manufacturing.

FOX-XP
The core value proposition of the automated FOX WLBI systems is playing out perfectly. Aehr’s lead AI production customer is shifting their entire AI accelerator testing protocol from package/system-level burn-in to 100% wafer-level burn-in using the FOX platform to protect device yields, structurally locking in high-margin automated system demand.

I should have bought Aehr when I got the chance. Demand is very real and it looks like Aehr is handling the scaling growth very well. Only upwards from here.

Investors are expecting a record quarter in terms of revenue. I regretted not buying them after last earnings, so I'm looking forward to what they bring to the table this time. EPS expectations: -0.01 Revenue expectations: $18.69M, which would be an 81% QoQ growth. Some other things I will look out for tonight - Silicon Photonics Optical Interconnects AEHR has recently secured back-to-back production orders, including a major follow-on order on July 9, 2026, for its systems targeting hyperscale data center optical interconnects. With AI workloads demanding faster data transfer, silicon photonics is AEHR's most immediate catalyst for secular growth and a key indicator of its penetration into the data center infrastructure stack. - Wafer-Level Memory Burn-in (WLBI) Adoption The company recently completed a benchmark with a global flash memory leader, positioning their equipment as a cost-effective alternative to traditional packaged-part testing. Breaking into the traditional memory space would drastically expand their Total Addressable Market (TAM) beyond their current niches, making any forward guidance on memory adoption critical for long-term valuation. - High-Power AI Processor Backlog Conversion Following a massive surge in bookings, including a record $41 million order from a lead hyperscale AI customer, AEHR's effective backlog hit an all-time high of $50.9 million going into Q4. The market is looking past the current revenue trough and wants to see how quickly management can convert these high-power AI processor orders (specifically for their newly acquired Sonoma systems) into recognized revenue. - Automated FOX-XP System Demand The fully automated FOX-XP is the linchpin of their hardware strategy, capable of testing up to nine 300mm wafers simultaneously. Because this system is highly complex and carries high margins, sustained demand for the FOX-XP dictates their ability to maintain pricing power and lock in Tier-1 customers across the AI and photonics sectors. - Insider Selling Dynamics Recent SEC filings from early July 2026 show a flurry of insider trading activity, with an estimated $42.8 million in shares sold by insiders over the preceding three months. While portions of this are tied to standard tax withholdings, the sheer volume warrants close scrutiny on the earnings call to ensure management's forward-looking confidence aligns with the current AI hardware hype."}}}, "rest_id": "2077007225910854086", "source": "Twitter Web App", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "26009", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}}, "rest_id": "2077136451175591988", "source": "Twitter Web App", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "13542", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

text:
$AEHR +27% $91.5 post earnings.   Net revenue was $18.8 million, compared to $14.1 million in the fourth quarter of fiscal 2025.

GAAP net income was $1.4 million, or $0.04 per diluted share, compared to GAAP net loss of $(2.9) million, or $(0.10) per diluted share, in the fourth quarter of fiscal 2025.

Outlook

Aehr Test Systems projects fiscal 2027 revenue of $130 mln to $150 mln. Representing 2.6x to 3.0x fiscal 2026 revenue !
Bookings were a record $60.7 million for the quarter.

Backlog as of May 29, 2026 was $80.6 million. Effective backlog, including bookings since May 29, 2026, is $100.6 million.

Total cash, cash equivalents and restricted cash as of May 29, 2026 was $116.5 million, compared to $37.1 million on February 27, 2026.
Company expects fiscal 2027 non-GAAP net income to be 18% to 22% of revenue

Company sees strong demand from AI, silicon photonics, and power semiconductor markets driving growth

text:
🚨 $AEHR CRUSHES Q4, massive beat, huge FY27 guide.

Here’s what we know:

📊 Q4 FY26:

• Revenue: $18.8M (vs $14.1M YoY)
• Non-GAAP EPS: $0.11 (vs -$0.02 est)
• Record bookings: $60.7M
• Cash: $116.5M (up from $37.1M last Q)

🚀 FY27 Guide:

• Revenue: $130M–$150M (160%-200% YoY growth)
• Non-GAAP margin: 18%-22%

⚠️ FY26 revenue itself fell YoY ($50.0M vs $59.0M), this is a backlog/inflection story, not a “great year” story.

Lead AI production customer shifting fully to wafer level burn in for accelerators, silicon photonics ramping, plus $8M in fresh SiC orders for EV programs last month alone. Diversification story is real.

If you’re long $AEHR, this backlog just gave you a 2027 roadmap most small caps would kill for.

#AEHR #Semiconductors #AI #Earnings
